failed_rules.message: LoanType must be Closed End Second for 2nd Lien program
US Citizens are not eligible for Select ITIN program.
FICO Score score 715 below minimum requirement 720
FICO Score score 715 below minimum 720 required for Primary Residence, SFR
Income Doc Type "1099: 12 Mo." is not a DSCR type - Investor DSCR requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Occupancy 'Primary Residence' not eligible for Foreign National. Available: Investment Property, Second Home
US Citizens are not eligible for Foreign National program - FAIL
Income Doc Type "1099: 12 Mo." is not a DSCR type - DSCR Supreme Program requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Income Doc Type "1099: 12 Mo." is not a DSCR type - DSCR Multi (5-8 Units) requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Income Doc Type "1099: 12 Mo." is not a DSCR type - Investor DSCR No Ratio requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL

specifications: Must be a tri-merge credit report from all three bureaus (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion)
Must identify all borrowers on the loan application
Must show credit scores from each reporting bureau
Must show complete tradeline details including payment history, balances, and credit limits
Must show mortgage history if any existing mortgages are reported
Must display all inquiries within the past 120 days
Must show public records including judgments, liens, and bankruptcies if any
Must show any active disputes, collections, or charge-offs
Must show any fraud alerts or OFAC flags if present
Must be dated within 90 days of the note date
reasons_needed: Credit score is required to validate program eligibility for Flex Supreme program
Tradeline and credit history requirements must be reviewed to confirm minimum tradeline standards are met
Credit report is needed to identify any credit events, inquiries, disputes, or housing history
Report validates borrower identity and supports underwriting decision

specifications: Must be valid, unexpired government-issued photo identification
Must show full legal name matching loan application (Veronica Marie Montes)
Must include photograph, date of birth, and expiration date
Acceptable forms: Driver's License, State ID, Passport, or Military ID

reasons_needed.reason: Borrower identity must be verified before credit, title, and compliance documents can be relied upon.
Required for Patriot Act/CIP compliance and borrower authentication.

specifications: Must be fully executed with borrower signature and date
Must include correct SSN for borrower Veronica Marie Montes
Must specify tax years 2023 and 2024 (12 months 1099 income verification)
Must include current subject property address: California SFR
Must authorize transcript request for Form 1099 income verification

reasons_needed.reason: Guidelines require fully executed and signed IRS Form-4506C for income verification.
1099 income documentation program requires IRS transcript verification of reported income.

specifications: Must be fully executed with all signatures from buyer (Veronica Marie Montes) and seller
Must show purchase price consistent with $411,000 (80% LTV on $328,800 loan)
Must identify subject property address in California
Must include all addenda, amendments, and counteroffers
Must show earnest money deposit amount and holder
Must include closing date and contingency deadlines

reasons_needed.reason: Purchase transaction requires fully executed contract to establish sales price, terms, and LTV calculation basis.
Contract terms needed to verify occupancy intent and subject property identification.

specifications: Must include 1099 forms for the most recent two (2) consecutive full tax years
Must be from the same income source for both years
Must show borrower name matching loan application
Must show payer name and payer TIN
Must show gross income amount
reasons_needed: 1099 income requires verification from the same source covering the most recent two (2) full years per guidelines
Income continuity and stability must be established for qualification
1099 income filed as Schedule C or E requires source documentation
document_requests.guideline_reference: EMPLOYMENT - STABILITY AND CONTINUANCE OF SELF-EMPLOYMENT

specifications: Must include complete personal tax returns for the most recent two (2) consecutive tax years
Must include all schedules and attachments
Must include Schedule C if filing self-employment income as sole proprietor
Must include Schedule E if reporting rental or partnership income
Must be signed and dated or include IRS transcript
Must show borrower name and SSN matching loan application
Must be included for each of the most recent two (2) tax years
Must show business name and principal business activity
Must show gross receipts and net profit/loss
Must be consistent with 1099 income reported
Must be part of complete tax return filing
reasons_needed: Self-employed borrowers must provide tax returns to calculate stable monthly income
1099 income filing as Schedule C or E requires full tax return review
Two years of returns required to verify income stability and continuance
All schedules required to properly calculate qualifying income
Schedule C is required to calculate net self-employment income
Business expenses must be deducted from gross 1099 income for qualification
Two years required to analyze income trend and stability

specifications: Must be from a disinterested third party (CPA, regulatory agency, licensing bureau, or Secretary of State)
Must verify current existence of the business
Must be dated within 30 business days of closing
Must show business name consistent with loan file
Alternative: Secretary of State listing reflecting current year registration
Alternative: Evidence of receipt of business income within 30 days supported by invoices or bank statements showing business deposits
reasons_needed: Self-employment business existence must be independently verified through disinterested third party
Verification must be within 30 business days of closing per guidelines
Required to confirm borrower's self-employment status and business continuity

specifications: Must cover most recent 2 consecutive months
Must include all pages of each statement
Must show borrower name as account holder
Must show financial institution name and logo
Must show account number (full or masked)
Must show beginning and ending balance for each month
Must show complete transaction history
Must be dated within 90 days of note date per NQMF guidelines
Must support funds to close calculation including 20% down payment, closing costs, and prepaids
Must identify any deposits exceeding 50% of qualifying income for potential sourcing
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require assets dated within 90 days of note date to verify sufficient funds
Purchase transaction at 80% LTV requires verification of 20% down payment (~$82,200)
Closing costs and prepaid items must be verified from acceptable sources
Reserve requirements must be supported by verified asset balances
Account ownership must be confirmed to match borrower
Large deposits may require sourcing documentation
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Assets Section - Loan files must evidence sufficient funds from acceptable sources for down payment, closing costs, prepaid items, debt payoff, and applicable reserves

specifications: Must show EMD amount as stated in purchase contract
Must show canceled check front and back OR wire transfer confirmation
Must show funds originated from verified borrower account
Must match EMD amount listed on purchase agreement
Must show payee as escrow/title company or seller as applicable
Must provide paper trail from borrower account to deposit recipient
reasons_needed: Purchase transaction requires verification of earnest money deposit
EMD must be sourced to borrower's verified funds
EMD amount credited toward down payment must be documented
Paper trail required to confirm acceptable source of funds
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Assets Section - Funds from acceptable sources for down payment

specifications: Must use FNMA Form 1004 (Uniform Residential Appraisal Report) for single-family residence
Must identify subject property address matching loan application
Must identify property type as single-family residence and confirm unit count
Must show appraised value supporting LTV/CLTV calculation
Must be dated within 120 days of Note date to avoid recertification requirement
Must include all pages, addenda, and certifications
Must include street map showing subject property and all comparables used
Must include exterior building sketch with dimensions and floor plan with GLA calculations
Must include original color photographs of front, street, and rear views of subject property
Must include interior photos of kitchen, all bathrooms, main living area, and any areas with deterioration or improvements
Must include comparable sales analysis with Sales Comparison Approach section completed
Must confirm property is in average or better condition (C1-C4 acceptable; C5/C6 ineligible)
Must confirm no fair or poor ratings and no health or safety issues
Must confirm property contains full kitchen and bathroom with minimum 400 sq ft
Must disclose any repairs, adverse conditions, or structural/deferred maintenance
Must include appraiser certification, statement of limiting conditions, and E&O insurance documentation
Must confirm property is designed and available for year-round residential use
Must confirm property represents highest and best use with no environmental hazards impacting marketability
reasons_needed: Collateral valuation is required for underwriting per NQMF guidelines
LTV/CLTV eligibility (80% LTV) depends on appraised value
Property type and condition must meet NQMF minimum property standards
Subject property identity must be consistent across the loan file
Appraisal must confirm property eligibility under Flex Supreme program
California property location requires verification of property characteristics

specifications: Must provide FNMA SSR (Submission Summary Report) from UCDP upload
Must show SSR score of 2.5 or less for appraisal acceptance
Must identify subject property matching appraisal report
Must address any flags or warnings requiring resolution
If SSR score exceeds 2.5, alternative review product required (House Canary AVM, Clear Capital Desk Review, or Field Review)
reasons_needed: NQMF Appraisal Review Process requires secondary appraisal product to support value
SSR score of 2.5 or less confirms appraisal validity without additional review products
Collateral risk assessment requires UCDP validation
guideline_references: APPRAISAL REVIEW PROCESS – 1-4 UNIT RESIDENTIAL PROPERTIES

specifications: Must be a Flood Certificate provided by FEMA-approved vendor
Must identify subject property address
Must indicate flood zone determination (Section B4 'Flood Zone' field)
Must indicate whether property is in Special Flood Hazard Area (SFHA) - Zone A or V
Must indicate NFIP community participation status if in flood zone
Must include Section B5 'No NFIP Map' indicator if applicable
Must include Section D 'Determination' confirming whether property is in FEMA flood zone containing letters A or V
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require flood determination to establish whether property is in SFHA
Flood insurance requirement depends on flood zone status per FEMA designation
Properties in Flood Zone A or V must be in FEMA-participating community for eligibility
Appraisal report must accurately reflect flood zone consistent with determination

specifications: Must be hazard insurance policy protecting against fire and standard extended coverage perils
Must include coverage for: fire/lightning, explosion, windstorm (including named storms), hail, smoke, aircraft, vehicles, riot/civil commotion
Must provide claims settled on replacement cost basis (actual cash value policies not acceptable)
Must name all persons holding title to subject property as named insured
Must include standard or union mortgagee clause (loss payable clause not acceptable)
Must show policy effective for at least 60 days after date of funding
Must include policy number, loan number, insurance company name, agent information
Must show effective and expiration dates, premium amount, coverage amounts and deductible
Must confirm property address matches note/security instrument
Must include notice of cancellation provision requiring written notice to named insured and mortgagee before cancellation
Must be signed and dated by insurance agent
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require hazard insurance on all properties to protect collateral
Coverage must meet minimum standards for fire and extended coverage endorsement
Replacement cost settlement basis required per guidelines
Mortgagee clause protects lender's security interest in collateral
guideline_references: MORTGAGEE CLAUSE REQUIREMENTS BY PROPERTY TYPE
NAMED INSURED FOR PROPERTY AND FLOOD INSURANCE

specifications: Must be issued by title insurer qualified to do business in California
Must identify borrower Veronica Marie Montes as buyer/proposed insured
Must identify subject property address and full legal description
Must contain 24-month chain of title history showing transfer dates, prices, and parties
Must disclose all liens, judgments, easements, encumbrances, and exceptions
Must list all title requirements that must be satisfied prior to closing
Must show effective date within acceptable recency window
Must indicate final title policy will be issued on 2006 ALTA standard form or acceptable equivalent
Must confirm first lien position for proposed mortgage
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require title insurance policy covering the loan that is valid and binding
Title must confirm ownership and vesting aligns with borrower on loan application
24-month title history required to review for inconsistencies or flipping activity
Liens and exceptions must be evaluated for impact on lien position and property value
Title insurer must be duly authorized and licensed in California per guideline requirements
Underwriter must verify no adverse title conditions that would affect loan collateral

specifications: Must be recorded deed showing current ownership of subject property
Must show grantor (seller) as current owner of record
Must include full legal description of property
Must show manner of vesting (e.g., sole owner, joint tenants, tenants in common, community property)
Must be consistent with chain of title in title commitment
For purchase transaction: must confirm seller has clear title to convey
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require final title policy vesting to reflect borrower names
Vesting deed confirms seller ownership and authority to convey property
Vesting must be reviewed for consistency with transaction structure
Chain of title must be verified against deed to ensure no breaks or inconsistencies
Required to establish insurable interest and proper conveyance at closing

specifications: Must be signed and dated by borrower Veronica Marie Montes
Must certify intent to occupy subject property as primary residence
Must acknowledge occupancy requirements and fraud penalties
Must be consistent with loan application and program selection (Flex Supreme)

reasons_needed.reason: Primary residence occupancy must be certified to support program eligibility and LTV limits.
Occupancy type determines documentation requirements, reserve requirements, and pricing.

specifications: Must cover period from January 1 of current year through most recent month-end
Must show business name matching loan file
Must show gross receipts and itemized expenses
Must show net profit/loss
Should be signed and dated by borrower or prepared by CPA if required by overlay
reasons_needed: YTD P&L required to assess current year income trend for self-employed borrowers
Supports income stability and continuance determination
Allows comparison of current year performance to prior tax return years

specifications: Must calculate monthly PITIA (Principal, Interest, Taxes, Insurance, Association dues)
Must show required reserve months per Flex Supreme program guidelines
Must identify assets used for reserves
Must show sufficient verified balance to cover required reserve months
Must exclude gift funds from reserve calculation per NQMF guidelines
Must account for any other financed properties if applicable
reasons_needed: Flex Supreme program requires reserves based on LTV and property characteristics
NQMF guidelines state gift funds may not be used to meet reserve requirements
Reserve calculation must be supported by verified assets
Primary residence purchase at 80% LTV requires minimum reserve verification
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Assets Section - Applicable reserves must be verified from acceptable sources
